* **Geometry Nodes**: A procedural node-based system in Blender for creating and manipulating geometry without destructive editing.
* **XPBD**: Extended Position-Based Dynamics, a numerical method used in computer graphics to simulate physical materials like cloth, hair, and soft bodies.
* **Principled BSDF**: A complex, all-in-one shader node in Blender that simulates various material properties based on physical principles.
* **Cycles**: Blender’s primary path-tracing render engine.

articles.title: 20+ Handpicked Fonts for Subtitles & Subheadings
articles.description: This roundup features 20+ fonts suited for subtitles and subheadings, spanning styles from geometric sans serifs to slab serifs, handwritten, and pixelated designs.
articles.one-liner: A collection of over 20 fonts curated specifically for subtitles and subheadings, focusing on high legibility and diverse visual styles.

summary.what: This list includes various typefaces ranging from geometric sans serifs like JUST Sans to pixelated options like Tiny Timmy, providing designers with alternatives for hierarchical text.

articles.title: Are we stuck in the typographic principles of the 20th century? Matter Of's new anti-design manual investigates
articles.description: New New Typography challenges traditional typographic principles through historical references, contemporary experiments, and contributions from nearly 100 designers, encouraging readers to rethink what typography can become.
articles.one-liner: Matter Of’s new book, New New Typography, challenges the rigidity of 20th-century design standards through a chaotic, experimental anti-manual.

summary.what: German design studio Matter Of collaborated with nearly 100 contributors, including Dinamo and Bye Bye Binary, to produce a 243-figure book that questions modern type design standards through ironic essays and experimental layouts.
summary.why: The project reflects a growing trend in creative fields to move away from the modernist obsession with 'clarity' and 'legibility' toward more malleable, expressive, and technologically unconventional forms of communication.

articles.deep-summary:
* Features 243 figures exploring typographic theory.
* Uses six distinct sans-serif typefaces to challenge the evolution of established fonts like Helvetica.
* Includes historical context dating back to 1602.
* Explores machine-generated and body-scanned text generation methods.
* Blends high-design theory with ironic, disruptive visual layouts.
articles.decoder:
* **New Typography**: A movement pioneered by Jan Tschichold in the 1920s that prioritized asymmetric layouts, sans-serif type, and extreme legibility over classical aesthetics.
* **Sans-serif**: A category of typefaces that do not have small lines or strokes (serifs) at the ends of character strokes.

articles.title: Accelerating Apache Spark Queries (and Iceberg Rust Development) with Apache DataFusion Comet
articles.description: DataFusion Comet speeds Spark reads on Iceberg by keeping Java planning while using Iceberg Rust for native Arrow execution. On a 3 TB TPC-DS benchmark, it accelerated 102 of 103 queries and cut total runtime by about 40%, while its use of Iceberg Java's Spark test suites has helped drive more than 40 Iceberg Rust pull requests.
articles.one-liner: DataFusion Comet boosts Apache Spark performance on Iceberg by offloading execution to Rust-based native engines while retaining Java-based query planning.

summary.what: Comet, donated by Apple to the Apache DataFusion project, intercepts Spark physical plans and converts them to DataFusion operations. It uses Iceberg Rust for efficient Arrow-based execution, achieving a 40% performance gain on 3 TB TPC-DS workloads.
summary.why: This represents a growing trend of replacing JVM-based query execution with native Rust runtimes to reduce resource overhead while keeping the mature Java ecosystem for complex query planning.
summary.takeaway: Test Comet with your existing Spark-on-Iceberg workloads to see if your CPU-bound or I/O-bound jobs benefit from the native execution path.

articles.deep-summary:
* Comet acts as an optimizer rule within Spark to replace JVM execution with DataFusion/Rust code.
* It leverages Iceberg Java for metadata and FileScanTask planning.
* DataFusion handles columnar Arrow data for faster processing.
* The system uses Iceberg Java's 10,000+ test suite as a differential-testing harness.
* Comet currently falls back to Java for table format version 3 or unsupported operations.
* Over 40 pull requests have been contributed back to Iceberg Rust through this testing workflow.
* It currently only accelerates table reads, not writes.
articles.decoder:
* **Differential testing**: A software testing technique where the same input is provided to two or more implementations of the same system to check for discrepancies in output.
* **Physical plan**: The final sequence of low-level operations (e.g., joins, scans) that a query engine executes to retrieve data.
* **Table format version 3**: A specific iteration of the Apache Iceberg storage specification that adds features like deletion vectors for row-level modifications.

articles.title: From OTEL to SLMs: Distilling Frontier Model Behaviour from Production Telemetry (45 minute video)
articles.description: OpenTelemetry traces can convert production AI-agent feedback into training labels for local 7B-13B coding models. Fine-tuned within hours, they can reach roughly 80-85% of frontier-model quality while lowering inference costs, preserving privacy, and scaling across the enterprise.
articles.one-liner: Production telemetry can be repurposed as high-quality training labels to fine-tune small language models that achieve near-frontier performance at a fraction of the cost.

summary.what: Using OpenTelemetry traces, organizations can distill behaviors from frontier AI agents into training data for 7B-13B parameter models, enabling private, local deployment.

articles.decoder:
* **OpenTelemetry (OTEL)**: A collection of APIs, SDKs, and tools used to instrument, generate, collect, and export telemetry data (traces, metrics, and logs) to analyze software performance.
* **SLM**: Small Language Model; typically referring to LLMs with significantly lower parameter counts (e.g., 7B-13B) designed for efficiency and local deployment.

articles.title: Exploring Hierarchical Interest Representation For Meta Ads Deep Funnel Optimization
articles.description: Meta compresses its graph of billions of users and advertiser entities into clusters of latent interests, enriches them with LLM-processed ad content, and embeds everything in one shared space where any user or entity can be scored against any other. Learning from broad graph structure instead of only rare conversion events gives the deep funnel a usable signal, and the embeddings feed Meta's ad ranking models like GEM and Andromeda.
articles.one-liner: Meta is using a transformer-based hierarchical graph encoder to map billions of users and ad entities into a shared, latent interest space.

summary.what: The system processes graph data with bias-aware attention kernels to create 'Bag-of-Meaning' tokens, enabling deeper personalization for its ad ranking models like GEM and Andromeda.
summary.why: This approach highlights a shift toward grounding ad-ranking models in multi-modal world knowledge (text/video) rather than relying solely on sparse click-through signals.

articles.deep-summary:
* Employs a heterogeneous, time-decayed graph connecting users, ads, and products.
* Uses LLMs to enrich entities with multimodal world knowledge.
* Implements memory-efficient 'FlexAttention' to apply graph-structural bias without full matrix materialization.
* Trains via self-supervised cross-view distillation to solve signal sparsity in the deep funnel.
* Discretizes embeddings into 'Bag-of-Meaning' (BoM) interest tokens for retrieval performance.
* Strict temporal data separation prevents future data leakage during model training.
articles.decoder:
* **Latent interest**: An inferred interest or preference not explicitly stated by a user but detected through behavioral analysis.
* **Deep funnel**: The later stages of the customer journey where a user is close to making a purchase or conversion.
* **Sinkhorn-Knopp**: An algorithm used in machine learning to enforce balanced assignment during clustering, preventing the model from collapsing all outputs into a single category.

articles.title: Data Modeling isn't Dead, You Just Stopped Doing It with Joe Reis (65 minute video)
articles.description: Data modeling is still essential, especially in the AI era, because clear structures, standards, and context help teams avoid bad data, wasted work, and constant firefighting. Modern data professionals need a flexible “mixed model” approach that uses the right modeling style for the problem rather than forcing everything into one method.
articles.one-liner: Data modeling remains a critical skill for avoiding 'firefighting' and technical debt in modern, AI-driven data architectures.

summary.what: Joe Reis argues that teams should adopt a 'mixed model' approach, selecting the most appropriate modeling strategy for specific use cases rather than abandoning structure altogether.

articles.title: Agents think in milliseconds, legacy infrastructure doesn't. LinkedIn, Walmart, and Zendesk shared how they closed the gap at VB Transform 2026
articles.description: Enterprise agents are constrained more by legacy infrastructure than model quality. LinkedIn pre-provisioned containers and shifted 80% of orchestration to deterministic code, Walmart governed duplicate internal agents, and Zendesk strengthened data pipelines for its 20B conversations. The lesson is to invest in evals, own the agent harness, and keep workloads portable across models.
articles.one-liner: Enterprise agent adoption is being throttled by legacy infrastructure gaps rather than model intelligence, according to LinkedIn, Walmart, and Zendesk.

summary.what: To bridge the gap, these firms are moving toward deterministic code for orchestration, strict internal governance of redundant agents, and portable, model-agnostic execution harnesses.
summary.why: This underscores that the primary challenge for enterprise AI is no longer the LLM, but the reliability and integration of the surrounding engineering systems.
summary.takeaway: Shift your focus from experimenting with new model weights to building a portable, deterministic evaluation and execution harness for your agents.

articles.decoder: * **Agent harness**: The scaffolding code that manages the lifecycle of an AI agent, including input sanitization, tool invocation, and error handling.

articles.title: AI Agents Need Data Product Context Not More RAG
articles.description: Enterprise AI agents need governed context, not just vector search: retrieval can find relevant passages, but it does not preserve source boundaries, business meaning, ownership, or usage restrictions. The proposed approach compiles one governed data-product model into multiple representations: graph, Markdown wiki, portable OKF bundle, and compact sidecars such as TOON/GCF. AI readiness depends on connected, trusted context and repeatable operations rather than catalog presence alone.
articles.one-liner: Enterprise agents need structured data-product context, not just vector search, to ensure reliability and preserve business logic.

summary.what: Dr. Jarkko Moilanen proposes compiling governed models into parallel representations—graphs, Markdown wikis, and compact sidecars—to provide agents with context that includes ownership, constraints, and source evidence.
summary.why: This signals a necessary evolution from 'search-based' AI to 'context-aware' AI, moving governance from the catalog level to the execution level.
summary.takeaway: Stop treating source documents as mere text chunks; start building explicit, evidence-backed business objects that define the context of your data products.

articles.deep-summary:
* RAG fails at scale because it lacks source boundaries, ownership info, and usage restrictions.
* Data products should be compiled into multiple formats (graph, YAML, Markdown, sidecars) to suit different consumer needs.
* The system should maintain three layers: source evidence, machine interpretation, and human-approved portfolio knowledge.
* Deterministic code should verify metadata against source evidence rather than relying on LLM-generated output.
* Context should be scoped to the specific agent task, not provided as an entire enterprise dump.
* Relationships in the graph should be 'evidence-aware', identifying if a link was extracted, inferred, or manually approved.
articles.decoder:
* **RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)**: An AI framework that retrieves data from external knowledge bases to ground the responses of LLMs.
* **TOON/GCF**: Compact, machine-readable sidecar formats used for shipping data-product metadata alongside assets.
* **OKF (Open Knowledge Format)**: A standard for bundling knowledge to ensure portability across local or offline environments.

articles.title: Introducing Apache Spark 4.2
articles.description: Apache Spark 4.2 adds metric views for defining business metrics, SQL vector similarity search, native geospatial types, and change data capture queries through a new CHANGES clause. Spark Connect and default Arrow optimized Python execution make the engine easier to reach. The release bundles over 1,900 commits from more than 260 contributors.
articles.one-liner: Apache Spark 4.2 prioritizes AI-native analytics by adding governed metric views, vector similarity search primitives, and first-class change data capture (CDC) support.

summary.what: Released by the Apache Spark community, version 4.2 includes native vector primitives, GEOMETRY types, and 'Auto CDC' for declarative pipeline maintenance, alongside improved Python integration through Arrow-optimized UDFs and Pandas 3 support.
summary.why: By moving semantic modeling and vector operations into the engine, Spark is positioning itself as a foundational component of the AI stack rather than just a batch processing tool, aiming to bridge the gap between lakehouse storage and AI-agent requirements.
summary.takeaway: Use the new 'SYSTEM.BUILTIN' and 'SYSTEM.SESSION' qualifiers to disambiguate function calls and prevent namespace collisions in your SQL scripts.

articles.deep-summary:
* Metric views allow defining business metrics once as governed objects accessible by SQL, BI, and AI agents.
* Vector search is now native with support for similarity functions, ranking (NEAREST BY), and normalization.
* Change Data Capture (CDC) is supported through a new CHANGES SQL clause and Auto CDC for automated SCD Type 1 processing.
* Python execution is now 'Arrow-first' by default, reducing serialization overhead between Spark and Python ecosystems.
* Spark Connect and improved PySpark compatibility simplify remote execution without needing a full JVM runtime.
* Real-Time Mode (RTM) now supports PySpark stateless streaming for low-latency applications.
* Data Source V2 (DSv2) adds standardized CDC and row-level DML performance improvements.
* Web UI has been updated to Bootstrap 5 with dark mode and better SQL visualization.
articles.decoder:
* **CDC (Change Data Capture)**: A pattern for identifying and tracking modifications to data so that downstream systems can react to changes.
* **Varlena**: Postgres's internal self-describing format for variable-length types (like TEXT, JSONB), used here to explain how Spark handles similar data structures.
* **SCD Type 1 (Slowly Changing Dimension)**: A data warehousing technique where old data is overwritten with new data, maintaining only the current state of a record.
* **Arrow**: A cross-language development platform for in-memory data, enabling high-performance columnar data transfer.

articles.title: Ontology Playground (GitHub Repo)
articles.description: Microsoft's Ontology Playground is a free, open-source tool for visually exploring, building, importing and sharing ontologies for Microsoft Fabric IQ.
articles.one-liner: Microsoft released an open-source visual playground for designing and testing ontologies intended for Microsoft Fabric IQ.

summary.what: The Ontology Playground is a static web application built with React 19 and Cytoscape.js that allows users to create, import, and share RDF/XML ontology diagrams with zero backend requirements.
summary.why: As AI agents require structured world models to function, Microsoft is providing developer tooling to help engineers map business logic into machine-readable knowledge graphs.
summary.takeaway: Use the web-based visual designer at microsoft.github.io/Ontology-Playground to draft and validate your data schemas before deployment.

articles.decoder:
* **Ontology**: A formal representation of knowledge as a set of concepts within a domain and the relationships between those concepts.
* **RDF (Resource Description Framework)**: A standard model for data interchange on the web, designed to represent graph data.
* **Fabric IQ**: A Microsoft service focused on AI-powered real-time intelligence and knowledge management.

articles.title: Postgres 19 Compression: from pglz to LZ4
articles.description: Postgres 19 plans to switch default TOAST compression from pglz to LZ4, while keeping the same unified compression framework across heap storage, TOAST, and B-tree indexes. Variable-length types like TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB are compressed automatically via varlena metadata, with rows targeting ~2 KB before spilling out-of-line to TOAST. LZ4 is much faster than pglz in tests, often compresses better, and still fails fast on incompressible data.
articles.one-liner: Postgres 19 is set to adopt LZ4 as the default TOAST compression algorithm, significantly outperforming the legacy pglz method in speed and efficiency.

summary.what: Crunchy Data reports that LZ4 will become the default for variable-length types (TEXT, VARCHAR, JSONB), offering 8x faster compression in tests while maintaining Postgres's fail-fast design for incompressible data.
summary.why: This transition marks a shift in Postgres toward modernizing its storage engine to align with current CPU and hardware capabilities, moving away from algorithms developed in the early 2000s.
summary.takeaway: If you have incompressible data causing indexing errors, verify your column size against the 510-byte limit (TOAST_INDEX_TARGET), as compression will no longer be the only bottleneck for B-tree index row size.

articles.decoder:
* **TOAST (The Oversized-Attribute Storage Technique)**: A mechanism in Postgres that transparently moves large, variable-length values out-of-line to a separate storage table.
* **pglz**: The legacy PostgreSQL-specific compression algorithm used since version 7.1.
* **Varlena**: The internal storage format for variable-length types in Postgres, containing a header that dictates whether data is compressed.

articles.title: Experience Graphs: The Data Foundation for Self-Improving Agents
articles.description: Trellis stores agent artifacts, rewards, search data, and causal history as persistent experience graphs accessible through SQL, graph, vector, and temporal queries. Meta found that reusing past experience matched performance about 10x faster and reduced successful-solution token costs by 52%, though excessive memory reuse reduced exploration.
articles.one-liner: Meta researchers propose treating agent search trees as 'experience graphs' stored in a database to enable cumulative agent learning.

summary.what: Trellis is a proposed data foundation that stores agent search traces, rewards, and causal history as queryable state, allowing agents to resume sessions, reuse subtrees, and extract training data through SQL/graph/vector queries.
summary.why: This signals a transition from viewing agent inference as disposable log-based processes to treating it as persistent, institutional data assets that support recursive self-improvement.

articles.deep-summary:
* Trellis manages agent state as a persistent, queryable Experience Graph (Causality, Executability, Reward).
* Architecture separates compute (stateless agents) from storage (durable database).
* Common operations like resume, reuse, and repair are treated as database access patterns rather than application logic.
* Enables 'vector-seeded graph expansion' to find and reuse high-performing past search trajectories.
* Training data extraction is performed via materialized views, eliminating the need for post-hoc scraping.
* Supports concurrency by managing tree statistics and backpropagation within the database transactional scope.
* Evaluation on 'KernelEvolve' showed 10x faster convergence and 52% lower token costs for optimized hardware kernels.
articles.decoder:
* **RSI (Recursive Self-Improvement)**: A process where an agent iterates on its own performance, using past outcomes to guide and improve future search and training.
* **MCTS (Monte Carlo Tree Search)**: A search algorithm used in decision-making and AI that builds a search tree incrementally by balancing exploration and exploitation.
* **Vellox**: Meta's open-source, high-performance vectorized execution engine.
* **DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)**: A technique to align LLMs to human preferences without requiring a separate reward model.

articles.title: From Weeks to a Day: How We Made LLM Evaluation Fast Enough to Iterate on
articles.description: Airbnb cut LLM evaluation cycles from weeks to under a day by caching generated references and judge scores, since over half of model outputs across candidates were identical strings and judge drift of about 1% per run. Small LoRA adapters with rank under 50 train in under an hour on a single GPU for same day fixes, and a final validation stage runs sampled traffic to catch failures.
articles.one-liner: Airbnb optimized LLM evaluation by caching generated references and judge scores, reducing turnaround times from weeks to under a day.

summary.what: By leveraging the fact that ~50% of LLM outputs are identical across candidates and using LoRA adapters for small, same-day fixes, the team maintained evaluation speed while catching regressions in production traffic.
summary.why: This reveals the necessity of engineering specialized caches for non-deterministic AI outputs, effectively treating LLM evaluation as a traditional software CI/CD pipeline.
summary.takeaway: Cache the results of your LLM evaluations using a hash of the input prompt and model parameters to prevent redundant judge calls when testing minor prompt variations.

articles.decoder:
* **LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation)**: A method for fine-tuning large models by freezing the base weights and injecting small, trainable rank-decomposition matrices.
* **Judge**: An LLM used to evaluate the output of another LLM, typically by scoring for quality, safety, or relevance.

articles.title: Dremio's Exit Is the Clearest Sign Yet That Lakehouse-Only Won't Survive AI
articles.description: Dremio's sale to SAP is presented as evidence that data platforms built around a single lakehouse will struggle with AI's demands for broad reach, freshness and unpredictable querying.
articles.one-liner: Dremio's acquisition by SAP is framed by competitors as a failure of 'lakehouse-only' data platforms to meet the complex demands of modern AI.

summary.what: The sale of Dremio, a data lakehouse company, is interpreted as a signal that the market prefers broader, integrated data platforms over single-purpose lakehouse architectures.
summary.why: This highlights a potential industry consolidation, suggesting that standalone lakehouse engines struggle to compete with platforms that offer richer governance and multi-modal query capabilities.

articles.decoder: * **Lakehouse**: A data architecture that combines the low-cost, scalable storage of a data lake with the data management and performance features of a data warehouse.

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI's favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks is reportedly raising $3 billion at a $188 billion valuation after successfully repositioning itself from a data platform into a major enterprise AI provider.
articles.one-liner: Databricks secured a $188 billion valuation by successfully rebranding its data infrastructure as a mission-critical platform for enterprise AI agents.

summary.what: Databricks is raising $3 billion in a round led by Coatue, bringing its valuation to $188 billion just five months after a $134 billion round. CEO Ali Ghodsi is prioritizing open-weight models like Z.ai’s GLM 5.2 and agentic harnesses like Pi to reduce internal engineering costs.
summary.why: This validates the strategy of pivoting from legacy big-data storage to 'AI-native' governance, proving that owning the underlying data layer is more valuable than building the models themselves.

articles.deep-summary:
* Databricks moved from big data analytics to AI orchestration with products like Lakebase (database for agents) and Unity (AI gateway).
* The company uses Omnigent to manage multi-agent workflows.
* Internal benchmarks show open-weight models like Z.ai’s GLM 5.2 now match proprietary models for complex coding tasks.
* Databricks found that the 'harness' (the agentic wrapper managing context) is as important as the model itself for controlling costs.
* The Pi harness outperformed competitors in context management, reducing unnecessary token usage.
* Rapid valuation growth reflects investor demand for companies that control the 'plumbing' of the AI ecosystem.
articles.decoder:
* **Lakebase**: Databricks’ proprietary database architecture specifically optimized for indexing and serving data to AI agents.
* **Open-weight models**: AI models where the weights (learned parameters) are publicly available, allowing developers to host and fine-tune them on their own infrastructure rather than relying on a closed API.
* **Harness**: A software wrapper around an LLM that handles prompt engineering, context window management, and task iteration to improve agentic performance.
